title: Saudi Stocks Hit 52-Week Highs, Yamama Cement Expands as Bitcoin Holds $64.5K
excerpt: TASI stocks surge to 52-week highs while Yamama Cement launches third production line; Bitcoin steadies at $64,489 amid crypto consolidation.
content:
Saudi Stocks Rise as Yamama Cement Eyes Expansion, Bitcoin Holds $64.5K

Global markets show mixed momentum on Sunday as equities inch higher and cryptocurrencies stabilize around key levels. Saudi Arabia's Tadawul index continues to attract attention with corporate expansion news, while digital assets demonstrate resilience despite minor pullbacks in some major tokens.

Saudi Arabia: TASI Stocks Hit 52-Week Highs

Two stocks on Saudi Arabia's Tadawul All Share Index (TASI) reached 52-week highs, signalling continued investor confidence in select equities. The movement reflects broader strength in the kingdom's equity market as companies deliver positive operational updates. Source: Argaam

Yamama Cement Ramps Production with Third Line Launch

Yamama Cement announced it will commence commercial operations of its third production line on July 1, 2026, positioning the company for increased output and revenue potential. This expansion signals confidence in cement demand and represents a significant operational milestone for the Saudi cement manufacturer. The move could support stronger earnings in coming quarters as capacity grows. Source: Argaam

Cryptocurrency Markets: Bitcoin Holds Above $64K, Ethereum Slips

Bitcoin continues to trade around $64,489, up 0.92% over the last 24 hours, demonstrating relative stability despite broader macro uncertainty. Ethereum declined slightly to $1,672.61, down 0.22%, while alternative coins showed mixed performance. Solana gained 0.46% to $68.06, and BNB climbed 0.84% to $611.63, suggesting selective strength among altcoins even as the broader crypto market consolidates.

MoneyTailors Daily Market Brief covers equity, commodity, forex, and crypto markets across North America, Europe, Asia, and Middle East markets. Published daily at 07:00 GMT.

title: Bitcoin $64.5K, Saudi Contracts Boost Markets as Equities Mixed
excerpt: Crypto holds steady with BTC up 0.96% while Saudi Arabia's 2P wins $13.8M contract and MEPCO closes property deed.
content:
Saudi Business Growth Amid Mixed Equity Performance

Global markets show modest crypto gains Sunday as Bitcoin holds above $64,500 while equity sectors display divergent momentum. Saudi Arabia's business sector captures attention with major contract wins and property developments, though some regional equities face headwinds. Cryptocurrency markets remain relatively stable with most major assets posting minor single-digit movements.

Cryptocurrency Markets Hold Steady

Bitcoin traded at $64,526.00, up 0.96% over the past 24 hours, demonstrating resilience in the broader crypto market. Ethereum slipped marginally to $1,673.47, declining 0.20% as altcoins showed mixed performance. Solana gained 0.81% to $68.12 while BNB rose 0.87% to $611.77, suggesting selective strength in layer-one blockchain assets. CoinGecko data reflects a market characterized by cautious optimism rather than significant directional conviction.

Saudi Business Sector Advances

2P Consulting secured a SAR 51.5 million government contract, signaling continued infrastructure investment and business activity in the Kingdom. This award demonstrates ongoing demand for specialized services in Saudi Arabia's development initiatives. Argaam reported the contract win as part of broader government spending priorities.

Real Estate and Property Development

MEPCO completed a deed transfer for property ownership, marking progress in the real estate sector's formalization and transaction volumes. Such transactions reflect ongoing activity in Saudi Arabia's property market despite broader market volatility. Argaam documented the finalized transfer as part of weekend business developments.

Equity Performance Concerns

Old Republic International (NYSE:ORI) posted disappointing performance metrics, underperforming market expectations despite its established market position. The insurance and financial services company's results reflect broader challenges in certain equity sectors. Seeking Alpha analysis highlighted the stock's struggle to gain traction amid market conditions.

content:
